summary: DHH announces the fourth release of Active Record in four days, version 0.8.1. He acknowledges he didn't follow the 'release early' part of the open source mantra but is making up for it with rapid iteration. He draws a parallel to Instiki, which had 9 releases in two months. The brief post credits open source culture for driving fast product improvement.
key_points: Active Record saw four releases in four consecutive days, demonstrating rapid iteration
DHH admits to not releasing early but compensates by releasing often
Open source development culture naturally drives rapid product improvement
money_quotes: So while I perhaps didn't follow the first part of "release early, release often", I am surely following the latter.
Open source has a habit of bringing that kind of rapid improvement to products.
key_insight: Open source development culture fosters a pace of iteration and improvement that proprietary development struggles to match.
